paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham took another wife, and her name was Keturah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And she bare him Zimran, and Jokshan, and Medan, and Midian, and Ishbak, and Shuah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jokshan begat Sheba, and Dedan. And the sons of Dedan were Asshurim, and Letushim, and Leummim.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the sons of Midian: Ephah, and Epher, and Hanoch, and Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham gave all that he had unto Isaac.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But unto the sons of the concubines, that Abraham had, Abraham gave gifts. And he sent them away from Isaac his son, while he yet lived, eastward, unto the east country.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And these are the days of the years of Abraham`s life which he lived, a hundred threescore and fifteen years.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham gave up the ghost, and died in a good old age, an old man, and full [of years], and was gathered to his people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Isaac and Ishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, which is before Mamre.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The field which Abraham purchased of the children of Heth. There was Abraham buried, and Sarah his wife.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And it came to pass after the death of Abraham, that God blessed Isaac his son. And Isaac dwelt by Beer-lahai-roi.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now these are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham`s son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah`s handmaid, bare unto Abraham.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And these are the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names, according to their generations: the first-born of Ishmael, Nebaioth, and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Hadad, and Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: These are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names, by their villages, and by their encampments. Twelve princes according to their nations.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And these are the years of the life of Ishmael, a hundred and thirty and seven years. And he gave up the ghost and died, and was gathered unto his people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they dwelt from Havilah unto Shur that is before Egypt, as thou goest toward Assyria. He abode over against all his brethren.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And these are the generations of Isaac, Abraham`s son. Abraham begat Isaac.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Syrian of Paddan-aram, the sister of Laban the Syrian, to be his wife.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Isaac entreated Jehovah for his wife, because she was barren. And Jehovah was entreated of him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the children struggled together within her. And she said, If it be so, wherefore do I live? And she went to inquire of Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah said unto her, Two nations are in thy womb, And two peoples shall be separated from thy bowels. And the one people shall be stronger than the other people. And the elder shall serve the younger.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when her days to be delivered were fulfilled, behold, there were twins in her womb.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the first came forth red, all over like a hairy garment. And they called his name Esau.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And after that came forth his brother, and his hand had hold on Esau`s heel. And his name was called Jacob. And Isaac was threescore years old when she bare them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the boys grew. And Esau was a skilful hunter, a man of the field. And Jacob was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now Isaac loved Esau, because he did eat of his venison. And Rebekah loved Jacob.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob boiled pottage. And Esau came in from the field, and he was faint.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Esau said to Jacob, Feed me, I pray thee, with that same red [pottage]. For I am faint. Therefore was his name called Edom.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob said, Sell me first thy birthright.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Esau said, Behold, I am about to die. And what profit shall the birthright do to me?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob said, Swear to me first. And he sware unto him. And he sold his birthright unto Jacob.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob gave Esau bread and pottage of lentils. And he did eat and drink, and rose up, and went his way. So Esau despised his birthright.
